---
name: Improve an existing guideline
about: Suggest clearer wording, labels, sections, sources, cross-links, splits, or merges for existing records.
title: "[Improve guideline]: "
assignees: ""
---

## Issue Type

Wording improvement, relabeling, split or merge, cross-linking, or source trail.

Use this template when friction from applying or retrieving an existing record points to a change that could improve the catalog for future users.

## Summary

Describe the improvement in one short paragraph. State how the current record was hard to retrieve, interpret, apply, cite, or distinguish from nearby records.

## Evidence From Use

- Task or chart context:
- Retrieval path:
- What was hard to find, ambiguous, duplicate, misleading, or under-sourced:
- Why this appears to be a catalog content issue rather than a tooling issue:

## Related Guideline Records

List verified guideline ids.

- `<guideline-id>`: `<title>`
  - Exact-read command:
  - Relevant section:
  - Why it partially applies, conflicts, duplicates, or needs revision:
  - Citation output:

## Suggested Catalog Change

Use the lines that fit.

- Rephrase title, description, or section text:
- Add or revise labels:
- Split or merge records:
- Add cross-links:
- Add or check source coverage:

## Public Disclosure Check

- [ ] No private user data.
- [ ] No local filesystem paths.
- [ ] No unpublished screenshots or session artifacts unless intentionally attached.
- [ ] Any referenced guideline ids were verified with exact reads.
- [ ] Source-backed records were checked with `chartcoach catalog cite` when references are discussed.

## Uncertainty

What should a catalog maintainer verify before changing the record?
